Subject: Aerial America - Oklahoma
Description: Viewing Oklahoma from the sky reveals a state filled with tall-grass prairies and man-made wonders; the Dust Bowl, Tornado Alley and buffalo-filled plains; it also shows a history steeped in tragedy and devastation.

Subject: Aerial America - Illinois
Description: A high-flying tour over Illinois; the Windy City, prairies and rivers; the state's dramatic history of great fires, crime and historic battles; Chicago's unique skyscrapers and the Lincoln Tomb.

Subject: Aerial America - Made in the USA
Description: A cross-country flight across America reveals the nation's most iconic industries, the people behind them and the stories of how they came to be.

Subject: Aerial America - North Carolina
Description: The Wright Brothers gave the state its slogan ``First in Flight``; civil rights leaders in Greensboro; NASCAR.

Subject: Aerial America - Alaska's Fire and Ice
Description: The world's largest volcanic island; Iceland's landscapes, forged by fire and ice; a tour over Alaska, the residents it attracts and the wildlife that thrives in these unique surroundings.

Subject: Aerial America - Pennsylvania
Description: A high-flying tour of Pennsylvania.

Subject: Aerial America - New York
Description: Beauty of the Empire State; Central Park; tallest skyscrapers; mighty rivers; 17th century explorer Henry Hudson.

Subject: Aerial America - Washington D.C.
Description: A soaring tour over Washington, D.C. explores the unique landscape, rich history and colorful story of the Nation's Capital.

Subject: Aerial America - New York City 24
Description: An aerial tour of New York City, spanning the city's five boroughs, capturing man-made and natural wonders; revealing the ways its citizens live, work and play.

Subject: Aerial America - The Wild West
Description: A journey over America's western landscape featuring ``wild`` history and stories of colorful characters.

Subject: Aerial America - Boston 24
Description: A day-long aerial tour of the New England city of Boston in Massachusetts; where Americans first rebelled against Britain by dumping chests of tea into the harbor and where rock band Aerosmith first made it big.

Subject: Aerial America - Tennessee
Description: From battlefields where hundreds died to Beale Street where musical legends were born.

Subject: Aerial America - Rhode Island
Description: A small state has a whole lot of history.

Subject: Aerial America - Oregon
Description: Oregon's Cascade Mountains; Columbia River waterway.

Subject: Aerial America - On the Water
Description: Taking flight for a bird's eye tour of America's rivers, lakes and canals.

Subject: Aerial America - Arkansas
Description: An aerial view of Arkansas, where settlers' westward journeys began and the scene of Bonnie and Clyde's most serious crime; a view of the land of raging rivers, vast wilderness and legends of the Wild West.

Subject: Ice Airport Alaska - Arctic Guardians
Description: A suspected toxic leak grounds a 737 at Anchorage International, engine trouble endangers the mid-air refuelling of the USAF's most advanced fighter jets in Alaska, and a discovery at airport customs busts a potential smuggling.

Subject: Ice Airport Alaska - Snow Bomb
Description: Record-breaking snow in Anchorage could shut down the airport; a novice pilot tests his nerve landing on a frozen lake; USAF aircrews battle the toughest Arctic survival training; a unique aircraft workshop Alaska-proofs a vintage.

Subject: Ice Airport Alaska - Heavy Lifting
Description: A large construction load, a massive repair, and a fallen soldier weigh heavily on the crews.

Subject: Ice Airport Alaska - Attack of the Yeti
Description: Bush aviators push the limit of their plane to deliver a two-ton generator. In Fairbanks, slippery runways threaten The Internation Ice Carving Championship and a high-tech airframe inspection on a Boeing 737 undercovers a poten.

Subject: Ice Airport Alaska - Moose vs Helicopter
Description: The first fly-by-wire helicopter is put through its paces, as test pilots harness freezing temperatures. In Anchorage, a jet is lifted for repairs. In Talkeetna, pilots fight ice to deliver supplies to a homesteader's cabin in a.

Description: The first fly-by-wire helicopter is put through its paces, as test pilots harness freezing temperatures. In Anchorage, a jet is lifted for repairs. In Talkeetna, pilots fight ice to deliver supplies to a homesteader's cabin in a.

Subject: Air Disasters - Runway Collisions

Subject: Air Disasters - Storming Out
Description: On its final approach to Charlotte, N.C., US Air Flight 1016 is engulfed in intense rain obstructing the view from the cockpit; the pilots abort the landing, but the aircraft crashes to earth, hurtling along a road and breaking apart.

Subject: Air Disasters - The Invisible Plane

Subject: Air Disasters - Southern Storm
Description: Pilots of Southern Airways Flight 242 must try to land on a Georgia highway after the engines die and the windshield shatters.

Subject: Air Disasters - Cracks in the System

Subject: Air Disasters - The Final Blow
Description: An Air Inter flight crashes into the Vosges Mountains while attempting to land killing most of the 96 passengers.

Subject: Air Disasters - Fight to the Death

Subject: Air Disasters - Terror Over Michigan
Description: After a Boeing 727's near-fatal nosedive over Michigan, investigators risk their own lives to determine what caused the aircraft to lose control.

Subject: Air Disasters - Explosive Proof
Description: The inside story of one of history's most infamous and complex investigations: the 1996 crash of TWA Flight 800.

Subject: Air Disasters - Missed Approach
Description: A plane crash lands in Guam after losing sight of the airport in a storm.

Subject: Air Disasters - Who's In Control
Description: Turkish Airlines Flight 1951 stalls and crashes less than a mile from the runway while approaching Amsterdam Airport Schiphol.

Subject: Air Disasters - Deadly Space
Description: In the skies above the Mojave Desert, the VSS Enterprise accelerates toward the outer reaches of Earth's atmosphere, guided by test pilots; the aircraft breaks apart and falls from the sky, killing the flight's co-pilot.

Subject: Air Disasters - Out of Control
Description: A simple error years prior causes the deadliest single-plane crash in history.

Subject: Air Disasters - Subtle Incapacitation
Description: The 2010 crash of Ethiopian Airlines Flight 409 sparks rumors of terrorism.

Subject: Air Disasters - Fatal Distraction
Description: Pilots of Eastern Airline Flight 401 try to determine why both landing-gear lights do not function on an L-1011, tragically distracting them.

Subject: Air Disasters - Deadly Inclination
Description: An Alitalia airliner crashes into a forested hillside just a few miles from a Zurich runway.

Subject: Air Disasters - Fatal Friction
Description: Human flaws cause three plane crashes in England, Colorado and Minnesota.

Subject: Air Disasters - Seconds to Survive
Description: On 21 February 2008, Santa Bárbara Airlines Flight 518 strays off course and flies into the side of a mountain shortly after takeoff.

Subject: Air Disasters - Death in the Arctic
Description: On 20 August 2011, First Air Flight 6560, a charter flight, crashes near its destination of Resolute Bay Airport, Resolute, Nunavut, Canada, in poor weather conditions; out of the fifteen passengers and crew members, only three survived.

Subject: Air Disasters - Who's Flying the Plane?
Description: A look at accidents and incidents where problems with the interface between crew members and onboard avionics are a factor, and the causes of those problems.

Subject: Air Disasters - Death from Above
Description: Mid-air tragedies are particularly horrifying when they occur over a community, and rain terror on the people below.

Subject: Air Disasters - Afghan Nightmare
Description: When a cargo plane crashes at a U.S. airfield in Afghanistan, investigators reach a conclusion that ultimately proves incorrect.

Subject: Ice Airport Alaska - Attack of the Yeti
Description: Bush aviators push the limit of their plane to deliver a two-ton generator. In Fairbanks, slippery runways threaten The Internation Ice Carving Championship and a high-tech airframe inspection on a Boeing 737 undercovers a poten.

Subject: Ice Airport Alaska - Moose vs Helicopter
Description: The first fly-by-wire helicopter is put through its paces, as test pilots harness freezing temperatures. In Anchorage, a jet is lifted for repairs. In Talkeetna, pilots fight ice to deliver supplies to a homesteader's cabin in a.

Subject: Air Disasters - A Wounded Bird
Description: A blade on the left-side propeller of Atlantic Southeast Airlines Flight breaks off; the imbalance of the rotating propeller causes the engine to partly tear itself from its mounting.

Subject: Air Disasters - Hudson River Runway
Description: US Airways Flight 1549 suffers a bird strike with a flock of Canada geese, causing both engines to fail.

Subject: Air Disasters - Hanging by a Thread
Description: The roof of an Aloha Airlines 737 tears off the plane at 24,000 feet in the sky.

Subject: Air Disasters - Disaster at Tenerife
Description: Two 747s collide on the runway of a small airport in the Canary Islands, resulting in history's deadliest air crash.

Subject: Air Disasters - Deadly Delivery
Description: UPS Airlines Flight 1354 clips the tops of trees and crashes into a hillside about one nautical mile short of the runway during final approach to Birmingham-Shuttlesworth International Airport in Alabama.

Subject: Air Disasters - Accident or Assassination
Description: A plane carrying Mexico's interior minister crashes into Mexico City's financial district.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Viking Sea
Description: The new Viking Sea cruise ship whisks guests away on a journey across the Arctic Circle and into the heart of Norway.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Paul Gauguin
Description: The Paul Gauguin offers five-star luxury while sailing near the coral reefs, mountainous islands and tropical playgrounds of French Polynesia.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Marco Polo
Description: This 50-year-old ocean liner offers off-season Baltic tours, battling unpredictable weather to deliver one-of-a-kind experiences.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Regal Princess
Description: Follow mega cruiser Regal Princess as she whisks guests to Germany, Russia, and Scandinavia in style.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Diamond Princess
Description: Japan's largest cruise ship brings East and West together on a cruise featuring cherry.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Carnival Vista
Description: The Carnival Vista is the new flagship for one of the world's largest cruise lines, carrying more than 4,600 passengers and boasting 10 restaurants, nearly 2,000 staterooms and a crew of 1,400.

Subject: Mighty Trains - Seven Stars of Kyushu
Description: A trip aboard one of the world's most expensive and sought-after train rides details the journey for both passengers and crew.

Subject: Mighty Trains - The Canadian
Description: VIA Rail's passenger train travels between Vancouver and Toronto on four-day, four-night journey that cater to everyone.

Subject: Mighty Trains - Thai Rail & Death Railway
Description: Travel aboard the colourful trains of the State Railway of Thailand and explore the famed Death Railway; the trains travel through ancient areas, historic tunnels, and raging rivers.

Subject: Mighty Ships - Norwegian Epic
Description: The Norwegian Epic is a billion-dollar showboat with more than 20 bars and restaurants and pool decks that transform into nightclubs.

Subject: Mighty Ships - Quantum of the Seas
Description: Filled with big toys, guests fly the trapeze, surf and skydive without ever leaving the world's most innovative cruise ship.

Subject: Mighty Ships - Crystal Serenity
Description: The world's top cruise ship, the Crystal Serenity visits Sydney harbor, the port of Melbourne, Bali, a large-scale furniture load in Semarang, Indonesia and the port of Singapore.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Azamara Journey
Description: Azamara Journey travels the rivers of Southeast Asia, delivering passengers to exotic cities and hidden treasures that larger cruise ships cannot reach.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Europa 2
Description: Take a cruise where the guests choose the ports to visit and the captain and crew work overtime to make it happen.

Subject: Mighty Planes - Antonov 124
Description: The world's largest serially manufactured cargo plane, the Antonov 124, was built to move Soviet military vehicles and equipment.

Subject: Mighty Trains - Rovos Rail
Description: South Africa's most luxurious train - Rovos Rail - travel from Pretoria to Cape Town in classic cars pulled by a series of steam, diesel and electric locomotives.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Viking Long Ship Gefjon
Description: Take a river tour through some of Europe's greatest cities--Budapest, Vienna, and Amsterdam--on Viking Longship Gefjon.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Sky Princess
Description: The Sky Princess carries passengers from Fort Lauderdale, Florida, to three Central American stops.

Subject: Mighty Trains - North Rail Express
Description: The highly-specialized North Rail Express container train travels from Narvik to Oslo, delivering cargo of fish as quickly as possible.

Subject: Mighty Trains - Belgrade to Bar Railway
Description: The prairies of Canada; the deserts of Australia; the cold of the Norwegian Arctic Circle; the steepest slopes of the mighty Swiss Alps.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Le Soléal
Description: New luxury liner Le Soleal navigates the uncharted waters of Indonesia to give her passengers one-of-a-kind encounters at exotic destinations.

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Ocean Endeavor

Subject: Mighty Cruise Ships - Roald Amundsen

Subject: Air Disasters - Northern Extremes
Description: Faced with the extreme conditions of the far North, three flights fall out of the sky leaving investigators to uncover the cause.

Subject: Air Disasters - Poor Piloting
Description: The crews of three doomed aircraft make catastrophic blunders.

Description: Sheldon tries to help Penny understand Leonard's work; Wolowitz gets jealous when Leonard starts hanging out with his new girlfriend.

Description: Sheldon and Koothrappali attend a university mixer; Leonard discovers that Penny believes in psychics.

Description: Guy is recruited to join a top-secret spy organization for the supernatural world; he must choose between his old life in the ordinary world and a new life as an undercover spy; Helen deals with trouble at the New York Mother House.

Subject: Talamasca: The Secret Order - A Wilderness of Mirrors
Description: Guy joins the Talamasca, leaving his old life as a lawyer behind; Helen trains him on the ins and outs of undercover work; Guy is sent to London on his first mission - to follow a mysterious hit man.

Subject: The Fifth Element (1997)
Description: The Earth is about to be destroyed by a huge ball of fire racing toward the planet. Cornelius, an old monk, knows how to stop the burning sphere with the help of Korben Dallas, a taxi driver and former secret agent and a woman named Leeloo.

Subject: Law & Order - Entrapment
Description: The FBI intervenes in the detectives' investigation of the attempted murder of an African-American Congress leader.

Subject: Law & Order - Legacy
Description: The assassination attempt on a family man forces detectives to reopen a 5-year-old case once ruled accidental.

Subject: Law & Order - Menace
Description: The detectives seek evidence to prove an apparent suicide is really a murder linked to an insurance scam.

Subject: Law & Order - Barter
Description: A case of mistaken identity results in the murders of two women; tensions arise between McCoy and Ross when a hunch helps McCoy nab a loan shark.

Subject: Hidden Habitats - Okavango
Description: Okavango is rich in diverse wildlife; a cycling inland delta.

Subject: Pumpkinhead (1988)
Description: An old woman summons a grotesque figure to punish teenage dirt bikers for killing a man's (Lance Henriksen) son.

Subject: The Return of the Living Dead (1985)
Description: Punk rockers, a cremator (Don Calfa) and medical-supply workers (Clu Gulager, James Karen) have a problem with zombies in Kentucky.

Subject: Night of the Living Dead (1990)
Description: For unknown reasons, the recently deceased are rising from the grave as flesh-hungry zombies. A small group of survivors flees from the undead horde and takes refuge in a remote farmhouse.

Subject: Tales From the Darkside: The Movie (1990)
Description: A doomed boy stalls a witch (Deborah Harry) with three tales: ``Lot 249,`` ``Cat From Hell`` and ``Lover's Vow.``

Subject: Bram Stoker's Dracula (1992)
Description: The vampire count (Gary Oldman) of Transylvania seeks his lost love (Winona Ryder) and the conquest of Britain by plague.

Subject: The Craft (1996)
Description: Teen misfits befriend a suicidal newcomer and strike back at their tormentors with witchcraft in Los Angeles.

Subject: The Lost Boys (1987)
Description: A woman and her sons move to a coastal California town for a fresh start, unaware that it is full of teenage vampire punks.

Subject: An American Werewolf in London (1981)
Description: A New York student (David Naughton) becomes the scourge of London after being bitten by a beast on the moors.

Subject: Law & Order - Matrimony
Description: Several suspects stand to gain from the untimely death of a very young woman's wealthy elderly husband.

Subject: Law & Order - Working Mom
Description: Evidence in a former police officer's murder leads to a suburban housewife represented by women's rights attorney Lanie Stieglitz.

Subject: Law & Order - D-Girl
Description: The detectives' murder case involving a high-powered studio executive leads them to Hollywood; back East, Ross learns the suspect has hired her ex-husband.

Subject: Law & Order - Turnaround
Description: The murder victim's ex-husband enters rehab just as police are about to arrest him; McCoy butts heads with a judge in Los Angeles.

Subject: Law & Order - Showtime
Description: Law clerks discover a tape that incriminates a new suspect; Ross tricks her ex-husband into discussing a plea.

Subject: Law & Order - Mad Dog
Description: McCoy tries to return a parolee to prison when he believes the serial rapist committed a murder after his early release.

Subject: Law & Order - Double Down
Description: McCoy tries to sidestep a plea bargain he made with a dangerous suspect in order to see justice done.

Subject: The Three Stooges - Slippery Silks
Description: The Stooges are carpenters who inherit a fancy dress boutique; they put on a fashion show with dresses they've designed based on furniture; during the show, the boys break a Ming vase.

Subject: The Three Stooges - They Stooge to Conga
Description: The trio encounters Nazis; guest Lloyd Bridges.

Subject: The Three Stooges - Uncivil Warriors
Description: Set in the civil war, the Stooges are spies for the north; they impersonate southern officers and infiltrate the enemy ranks to get valuable information.

Subject: Bones - The Murder of the Meninist
Description: The team investigates the death of a men's rights organization founder who may have been the victim of domestic abuse; Brennan loses her temper during an interrogation; Angela and the team struggle with Hodgins' bitterness.

Subject: Bones - The Monster in the Closet
Description: The team hunts for a serial killer who appears to have sinister interactions with the victims' bodies before dumping them; Booth and Aubrey consult a behavioral analyst to help find clues to the killer's identity.

Subject: Bones - The Last Shot at a Second Chance
Description: A suspect in the murder of a convicted felon who had been released to a halfway house has ties to Booth; Brennan testifies at an FBI hearing about her assault of an unarmed suspect from a previous case.

Subject: Bones - The Fight in the Fixer
Description: The team investigates the death of a private investigator found frozen after being dumped in the Potomac; Aubrey finds shocking information connected to his father's whereabouts.

Subject: Bones - The Strike in the Chord
Description: The team investigates the death of an a cappella singer whose body was found in a lab rat; Hodgins applies for an experimental nerve regeneration study; Booth tries to plan a summer trip with Parker; a new intern joins the lab.

Subject: Daybreakers (2009)
Description: Faced with a critical blood shortage, a vampire hematologist joins forces with humans to perfect a cure for his problem.

Subject: 28 Days Later (2002)
Description: Survivors (Cillian Murphy, Noah Huntley, Naomie Harris) try to stay a step ahead of vicious, virus-infected humans that have overrun London.
A man who specialises in debunking paranormal occurrences checks into the fabled room 1408 in the Dolphin Hotel. Soon after settling in, he confronts genuine terror.

Subject: Ghost Ship (2002)
Description: Members (Julianna Margulies, Ron Eldard) of a salvage crew and a pilot (Desmond Harrington) board a haunted vessel found floating in the Bering Sea.

Subject: Thirteen Ghosts (2001)
Description: A widower (Tony Shalhoub), his daughter, his son and others become trapped in a house with vengeful spirits.

Subject: Law & Order - We Like Mike
Description: After being a suspect himself, a key witness becomes uncooperative with prosecutors when his wedding is ruined by his arrest.

Subject: Law & Order - Passion
Description: McCoy and Ross must sort it out when the death of an editor points to a scorned girlfriend but might be a cover-up.

Subject: Law & Order - Past Imperfect
Description: The death of a former cover girl leads detectives to unearth secrets the woman was desperately trying to hide.

Subject: Law & Order - Terminal
Description: Probing an anti-Semitic shooting spree at a posh fund-raiser, Briscoe and Curtis unearth more-personal motives.

Subject: Law & Order - Thrill
Description: A delivery man's route leads detectives to his killers; prosecutors try to strike a deal when a confession is protected in the name of religion.

Subject: Law & Order - Denial
Description: The detectives follow the trail of a stolen credit card to locate two teens they suspect may have killed their newborn baby.

Subject: Law & Order - Navy Blues
Description: Briscoe and Curtis crack a killing ring during the investigation of a Navy officer's murder; McCoy and Ross fight for prosecution authority.

Subject: The Crazies (2010)
Description: A lawman (Timothy Olyphant) and his wife (Radha Mitchell) and two companions fight to make it out of town alive after an unknown toxin turns ordinary citizens into bloodthirsty lunatics.

Subject: Thirteen Ghosts (2001)
Description: A widower (Tony Shalhoub), his daughter, his son and others become trapped in a house with vengeful spirits.

Subject: Final Destination 2 (2003)
Description: Death returns to claim the lives of those who did not die in a horrible highway calamity as they were meant to do.

Subject: Final Destination 3 (2006)
Description: A high-school senior (Mary Elizabeth Winstead) and her friends must deal with repercussions of cheating death when they survive a terrible roller-coaster accident.

Subject: The Final Destination (2009)
Description: A horrifying premonition saves a young man (Bobby Campo) and his friends from death during a racetrack accident, but terrible fates await them nonetheless.

Subject: Ghost Ship (2002)
Description: Members (Julianna Margulies, Ron Eldard) of a salvage crew and a pilot (Desmond Harrington) board a haunted vessel found floating in the Bering Sea.

Subject: House of Wax (2005)
Description: Friends (Elisha Cuthbert, Chad Michael Murray) become stranded in a town where murderous twins entomb their victims in wax.

Subject: House on Haunted Hill (1999)
Description: A theme-park mogul (Geoffrey Rush) invites five guests to an abandoned asylum and offers $1 million to anyone who stays there all night.

Subject: Criminal Minds - I Love You, Tommy Brown
Description: Multiple married couples fostering young children are murdered in Seattle; Garcia worries that Kevin is about to propose.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Foundation
Description: When a young boy is found in the Arizona desert and another goes missing, the team searches for the kidnapper; Morgan draws on his difficult past to connect with the found child.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Heathridge Manor
Description: The team investigates a series of gothic, ritualistic murders in Oregon.

Subject: Criminal Minds - The Company
Description: When Morgan's sister sees a woman who looks like their presumed-dead cousin, the BAU reopens the case of her disappearance.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Divining Rod
Description: After an infamous serial murderer is executed, women in the same Oklahoma town are attacked by a copycat; Prentiss gets nervous about making a major real estate purchase.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Profiling 101
Description: The team leads a class of college students through the nearly 20-year chase for a serial killer.

Subject: NCIS - Nature of the Beast
Description: After a firefight and a concussion wreak havoc on Tony's mind, Tony needs Gibbs to help him regain his memories of the investigation.

Subject: NCIS - Restless
Description: When a beloved Marine is fatally stabbed just after returning from Afghanistan, the team makes a surprising discovery about his past.

Subject: NCIS - The Penelope Papers
Description: NCIS discovers a connection between McGee's grandmother (Lily Tomlin) and the murder of a Navy lieutenant.

Subject: NCIS - Enemy on the Hill
Description: An investigation begins after a contract assassin targets a Navy lieutenant commander; information about Abby's family emerges after tests for a kidney donation.

Subject: NCIS - Safe Harbor
Description: The team searches an abandoned cargo vessel for a killer after a coast guard officer is gunned down; McGee and Tony search for Gibbs' perfect match.

Subject: NCIS - Thirst
Description: Evidence reveals that a Navy lieutenant was killed by being force-fed too much liquid; Gibbs meets Ducky's new romantic interest (Cheryl Ladd).

Subject: Cujo (1983)
Description: Bitten by a rabid bat, a huge dog traps a Maine woman (Dee Wallace) and her young son (Danny Pintauro) in their Ford Pinto.

Subject: Candyman (1992)
Description: A professor's (Xander Berkeley) wife (Virginia Madsen) links a local legend to a Chicago serial killer (Tony Todd) fitted with a hook.

Subject: Fright Night (1985)
Description: Horror-film junkie Charley Brewster becomes convinced that a reclusive new neighbor is a vampire. Charlie has no choice but to turn to the only person who could possibly help: washed-up television vampire killer Peter Vincent.

Subject: Creepshow (1982)
Description: Five Stephen King tales inspired by 1950s comic books include a nagged professor (Hal Holbrook) and a tycoon (E.G. Marshall) with cockroaches.

Subject: Poltergeist (1982)
Description: A suburban couple's (Craig T. Nelson, JoBeth Williams) little girl is drawn from her bedroom into a nightmarish other dimension.

Subject: The Exorcist (1973)
Description: When a mysterious entity possesses a young girl, her mother seeks the help of two Catholic priests to save her life.

Subject: Poltergeist (1982)
Description: A suburban couple's (Craig T. Nelson, JoBeth Williams) little girl is drawn from her bedroom into a nightmarish other dimension.

Subject: Bones - The Cowboy in the Contest
Description: Booth and Brennan go under cover at an Old West-style shooting competition; Angela and Hodgins talk about the future; Cam and Sebastian grow closer.

Subject: Bones - High Treason in the Holiday Season
Description: A political journalist is murdered after publishing an article regarding corruption in the NSA; Hodgins' paranoia increases as members of the team realize they are being monitored; Brennan plans a surprise for Booth; a Thanksgiving celebration.

Subject: Bones - The Promise in the Palace
Description: An escape artist's corpse is found in the woods; Booth and Brennan try to decide if the tooth fairy should visit Christine; Angela works on her photography with Sebastian Kohl (Gil Darnell).

Subject: Bones - The Senator in the Street Sweeper
Description: Two sanitation workers (Sal Vulcano and James Murray) find a U.S. senator's corpse in a street sweeper; Aubrey looks into Jessica's past.

Subject: College Football - Georgia Tech at Duke
Description: The No. 12 Yellow Jackets duke it out with the Blue Devils. Georgia Tech improved to 6-0 for the first time since 2011 after winning the Battle of the Techs with Virginia Tech on Oct. 11. Duke has scored 128 points during its three-game win streak.

Description: A look at some of the Louisville Cardinal sports traditions and its environment for watching the game.

Subject: College Football - Louisville at Miami

Subject: College Football - Virginia at North Carolina
Description: The No. 16 Cavaliers (6-1, 3-0 ACC) visit Bill Belichick and the Tar Heels (2-4, 0-2). The teams have combined to average 68 points over the last seven meetings after North Carolina prevailed 41-14 in 2024. Virginia has won its last five games.

Subject: College Football - NC State at Pittsburgh
Description: The Panthers (5-2, 3-1 ACC) host the Wolfpack (4-3, 1-2). The series is tied 5-5-1. Pittsburgh rides a three-game win streak after a 30-13 decision at Syracuse. NC State had last week off after falling at then-No. 16 Notre Dame 36-7 on Oct. 11.

Subject: College Football - Boston College at Louisville
Description: The Eagles fly with the No. 19 Cardinals. Louisville pushed its way into the AP Top 25 after a 24-21 upset at then-No. 2 Miami on Oct. 17. Miller Moss (LOU) has created 10 TDs across his past three games. Boston College aims to snap a six-game slide.

Subject: ACC Traditions - Georgia Tech
Description: The Ramblin' Wreck, a 1930 Ford Model A, is one of the most time-honored traditions in college football as Yellow Jacket fans cheer the Sports Coupe and the Georgia Tech team onto the field.

Description: The ACC-leading No. 7 Yellow Jackets (7-0, 4-0) host the Orange (3-4, 1-3) in Atlanta. Syracuse squeaked out a 31-28 victory over Georgia Tech last season. The Orange are 0-3 since starting quarterback Steve Angeli went down for the season.

Subject: NFL Football - Baltimore Ravens at Miami Dolphins
Description: The Ravens visit the Dolphins. The clubs combined to score 155 points in splitting the last two meetings after Baltimore prevailed 56-19 in 2023. Miami is 1-2 at home this season. The Ravens are 0-2 on the road, falling to Buffalo and Kansas City.

Subject: Super Bowl Classics - XLV: Green Bay Packers vs. Pittsburgh Steelers

Subject: College Football - Ole Miss at Georgia
Description: The No. 5 Rebels (6-0, 3-0 SEC) and No. 9 Bulldogs (5-1, 3-1) play a high-stakes game between the hedges. Ole Miss has won two of the last three meetings after a 28-10 victory in 2024, but Georgia won the last six get-togethers at Sanford Stadium.
